title: Blacksmith Metal Knowledge
summary: Metal types, properties, crafting difficulty, quality levels
content: Blacksmiths know all metal types: Iron, Dull Copper, Shadow Iron, Copper, Bronze, Gold, Agapite, Verite, and Valorite. Each metal has specific properties including color, durability, crafting difficulty, and quality level. Valorite is the highest quality metal, requiring Blacksmithy skill 90-100. Verite requires 70-89 skill, Gold and Agapite require 50-69 skill, Copper and Bronze require 30-49 skill, and Iron requires 0-29 skill. Metal rarity affects availability and market value. Each metal has distinct visual characteristics and performance properties that affect the final crafted item's quality and durability.

title: Blacksmith Weapon Knowledge
summary: All weapon types, damage types, skill requirements
content: Blacksmiths know all weapon types: Swords (Longsword, Broadsword, Katana, Scimitar, Viking Sword, Cutlass, Rapier, War Fork, Kryss), Maces and Hammers (War Hammer, Mace, Maul), Axes (War Axe, Battle Axe, Axe, Double Axe, Executioner's Axe, Large Battle Axe, Two Handed Axe, Halberd, Bardiche), Polearms (Scythe, Pitchfork, Spear, Short Spear), Bows (Bow, Crossbow, Heavy Crossbow, Composite Bow, Repeating Crossbow), Throwing weapons (Boomerang, Cyclone, Glaive), and Tools (Butcher Knife, Skinning Knife, Dagger, Cleaver, Hatchet, Pickaxe, Club, Hammer). All weapons have three damage types: Slashing, Piercing, and Bludgeoning. Different weapons require different combat skills: Swordsmanship for swords, Macing for maces and hammers, Fencing for rapiers and daggers, and Archery for bows. Skill requirements range from 0-20 for basic weapons, 21-50 for intermediate, 51-70 for advanced, and 71-100 for master-level weapons.

title: Blacksmith Armor Knowledge
summary: All armor types, protection values, weight requirements
content: Blacksmiths know all armor types: Plate armor (Plate Helm, Plate Gorget, Plate Chest, Plate Arms, Plate Gloves, Plate Legs), Chain armor (Chain Coif, Chainmail Tunic, Chain Legs), Leather armor (Leather Cap, Leather Gorget, Leather Gloves, Leather Arms, Leather Legs, Leather Tunic), Studded armor (Studded Gorget, Studded Gloves, Studded Arms, Studded Legs, Studded Bustier), Bone armor (Bone Helm, Bone Armor, Bone Gloves, Bone Arms, Bone Legs), and Ringmail (Ringmail Tunic, Ringmail Legs, Ringmail Sleeves). Shield types include Buckler, Bronze Shield, Heater Shield, Metal Shield, Metal Kite Shield, Wooden Shield, and Wooden Kite Shield. Each armor type has different protection values, weight requirements, and strength requirements. Plate armor provides the highest protection but is heaviest, while leather is lightest but offers less protection. Armor weight affects movement speed and requires sufficient strength to wear effectively.

title: Blacksmith Crafting Requirements
summary: Blacksmithy skill levels, crafting requirements, related skills
content: Blacksmiths understand Blacksmithy skill requirements: 0-29 skill allows crafting basic iron items, 30-49 skill enables copper and bronze items, 50-69 skill allows gold and agapite items, 70-89 skill enables verite items, and 90-100 skill allows valorite items (highest tier). Mining skill (0-100) is required to obtain ore from veins. Tinkering skill is required for some complex items with moving parts. Carpentry skill is needed for weapon handles and wooden shield components. Tailoring skill may be required for some armor components that use fabric or leather. The quality of crafted items depends on the blacksmith's skill level, with higher skill producing better quality items. Material availability and skill level determine what can be crafted.

title: Weapon Skill Requirements
summary: Combat skill requirements for weapons, skill levels, effectiveness
content: Blacksmiths understand weapon skill requirements: Swordsmanship (0-100) is required for swords and affects sword damage and accuracy. Macing (0-100) is required for maces, hammers, mauls, and war hammers. Fencing (0-100) is required for rapiers, war forks, kryss, and daggers. Archery (0-100) is required for all bow types and affects bow damage and accuracy. Tactics skill (0-100) increases overall damage output for all weapons. Anatomy skill (0-100) increases damage output and healing effectiveness. Healing skill (0-100) is required for using bandages. Skill levels are categorized: 0-20 for basic proficiency, 21-50 for intermediate, 51-70 for advanced, and 71-100 for master level. Higher skill levels provide better damage, accuracy, and special move availability.

title: Strength Requirements for Weapons and Armor
summary: Strength requirements, weight, encumbrance mechanics
content: Blacksmiths understand strength requirements: Weapons have weight that affects how effectively they can be wielded. Heavier weapons require more strength to use effectively. Armor has weight requirements - heavier armor like plate requires more strength than lighter armor like leather. Strength directly affects damage output with melee weapons. Over-encumbrance occurs when carrying too much weight, causing movement penalties and reduced effectiveness. Each piece of equipment has a weight value, and total encumbrance is the sum of all equipped items and inventory. Sufficient strength is required to wear heavy armor sets without penalties. Strength also affects how much can be carried before becoming over-encumbered.

title: Item Repair and Maintenance
summary: Repair mechanics, durability, costs, when items become unrepairable
content: Blacksmiths understand item repair: All weapons and armor have durability that decreases with use. Items can be repaired using Blacksmithy skill, with repair success rate depending on skill level. Repair costs are based on item value and condition. Items become unrepairable when durability reaches zero or when they are too damaged. Higher quality items (valorite, verite) are more expensive to repair but last longer. Repair skill requirements match crafting requirements - higher tier items require higher skill to repair. Regular maintenance prevents items from becoming unrepairable. Some items may require special materials or tools for repair. The condition of an item affects its effectiveness in combat or use.

title: Merchant Trade Knowledge
summary: Trade goods, market values, supply and demand patterns
content: Merchants understand all item categories and their typical market values. They track supply and demand patterns, knowing when items are in high demand or oversupplied. Quality assessment is crucial - merchants can evaluate item condition, rarity, and collectibility. Market prices fluctuate based on availability, season, and regional demand. Merchants know which cities specialize in which goods and where to find the best deals. They understand trade routes and transportation costs. Bulk pricing and trade agreements affect profitability. Merchants stay informed about market trends and can advise on investment opportunities.

title: Currency and Economics
summary: Gold piece values, pricing, trade agreements, bulk pricing
content: Merchants understand currency: Gold pieces are the standard currency of Britannia. Merchants know how to assess fair prices based on item value, condition, and market conditions. Trade agreements and contracts can affect pricing for bulk purchases. Seasonal price variations occur based on availability and demand cycles. Merchants understand exchange rates, profit margins, and how to negotiate favorable deals. They know when to buy low and sell high. Currency values remain stable, but item values fluctuate. Merchants track economic trends and can predict price changes based on supply and demand.

title: Item Appraisal and Value Assessment
summary: How to assess item value, condition factors, rarity, historical significance
content: Merchants excel at item appraisal: They assess item value based on condition, quality, rarity, and historical significance. Condition factors include durability, wear, and damage. Rarity affects value - unique or rare items command higher prices. Historical significance increases value for items with provenance or legendary status. Merchants can identify forgeries and assess authenticity. They understand collectibility and what makes items valuable to different buyers. Market demand affects appraisal - items in high demand fetch better prices. Merchants consider all factors when determining fair market value.

title: Guard Combat Skills Knowledge
summary: All combat skills, skill levels, mechanics, effectiveness
content: Guards understand all combat skills: Swordsmanship (0-100) affects sword damage and accuracy. Macing (0-100) affects mace and hammer damage. Fencing (0-100) affects fencing weapon damage. Archery (0-100) affects bow damage and accuracy. Tactics (0-100) increases overall damage output for all weapons. Anatomy (0-100) increases damage output and healing effectiveness. Healing (0-100) is required for self-healing with bandages. Parrying (0-100) increases chance to block incoming attacks. Resisting Spells (0-100) reduces magical damage taken. Skill levels determine effectiveness: 0-20 is basic, 21-50 is intermediate, 51-70 is advanced, and 71-100 is master level. Higher skills provide better damage, accuracy, defense, and special move availability.

title: Combat Mechanics and Damage Types
summary: Damage types, armor protection, weapon stats, critical hits
content: Guards understand combat mechanics: Damage types include Physical (Slashing, Piercing, Bludgeoning), Fire, Cold, Poison, and Energy. Armor provides protection against physical damage, with different armor types offering different protection values. Plate armor provides highest protection, followed by chain, studded, leather, and cloth. Weapon speed and damage are inversely related - faster weapons do less damage per hit, slower weapons do more. Critical hits can occur with high skill and luck, dealing increased damage. Special moves and abilities become available at higher skill levels. Weapon effectiveness varies against different armor types. Guards know which weapons work best against which threats.

title: Combat Tactics and Strategy
summary: Formation fighting, defensive strategies, weapon selection
content: Guards understand tactical combat: Formation fighting allows coordinated defense and offense. Defensive strategies include proper positioning, shield usage, and armor selection. Weapon selection depends on the enemy - different weapons are effective against different threats. Armor selection balances protection with mobility - heavy armor protects but slows movement, light armor allows faster movement but less protection. Guards know when to engage and when to retreat. They understand threat assessment and prioritize targets. Tactical knowledge includes terrain advantage, choke points, and coordinated attacks. Guards train in group tactics and individual combat techniques.

title: Healer Herbs and Reagents Knowledge
summary: All herbs and reagents, properties, uses, locations
content: Healers know all herbs and reagents: Black Pearl, Blood Moss, Garlic, Ginseng, Mandrake Root, Nightshade, Spider's Silk, and Sulfurous Ash. Each reagent has specific properties and uses in healing and spellcasting. Black Pearl is used in various spells and potions. Blood Moss is essential for healing magic. Garlic has protective properties. Ginseng aids in stamina and energy. Mandrake Root is powerful but dangerous. Nightshade is used in poisons and cures. Spider's Silk has binding properties. Sulfurous Ash is used in fire-based magic and potions. Healers know where to find each reagent in the wild. Reagent combinations create different effects. Fresh reagents are more potent than dried ones.

title: Healer Potion Knowledge
summary: All potion types, effects, crafting requirements
content: Healers know all potion types: Agility Potion and Greater Agility Potion increase dexterity and movement speed. Cure Potion and Greater Cure Potion remove poison. Explosion Potion and Greater Explosion Potion create area damage. Heal Potion and Greater Heal Potion restore health. Night Sight Potion provides vision in darkness. Poison Potion and Greater Poison Potion inflict poison damage. Refresh Potion and Greater Refresh Potion restore stamina. Strength Potion and Greater Strength Potion increase physical strength. Greater versions are more potent but require higher Alchemy skill to craft. Potions require specific reagent combinations. Healers understand potion effects, durations, and when to use each type.

title: Healing Mechanics and Skills
summary: Healing skill, Anatomy, Veterinary, bandages, poison types
content: Healers understand healing mechanics: Healing skill (0-100) affects bandage success rate and healing amount. Anatomy skill (0-100) increases healing effectiveness and damage assessment. Veterinary skill (0-100) is required for healing pets and animals. Bandages are the primary healing tool, with success rate depending on Healing and Anatomy skills. Higher skills provide faster and more effective healing. Poison types include Lesser, Regular, Greater, Deadly, and Lethal - each requiring different cure methods. Resurrection mechanics allow bringing the dead back to life. Healers know first aid techniques and emergency treatment. Healing effectiveness increases with skill level and proper technique.

title: Diseases and Afflictions
summary: Poison levels, disease types, cure requirements, prevention
content: Healers understand diseases and afflictions: Poison levels range from Lesser to Lethal, each requiring different cure methods. Lesser poison is easily cured, while Lethal poison requires immediate and powerful treatment. Disease types include various ailments with different symptoms and treatments. Cure requirements vary - some require specific potions, others need magical healing, and some need time and rest. Prevention methods include proper hygiene, avoiding contaminated areas, and protective measures. Healers know symptoms of each affliction and can diagnose conditions. Treatment must match the severity - wrong treatment can worsen conditions. Healers understand the progression of diseases and when intervention is critical.

title: Mage Spell Circles Knowledge
summary: All 8 spell circles, spell lists, progression
content: Mages know all eight spell circles: First Circle includes Clumsy, Create Food, Feeblemind, Heal, Magic Arrow, Night Sight, Reactive Armor, and Weaken. Second Circle includes Agility, Cunning, Cure, Harm, Magic Trap, Magic Untrap, Protection, and Strength. Third Circle includes Bless, Fireball, Magic Lock, Poison, Telekinesis, Teleport, Unlock, and Wall of Stone. Fourth Circle includes Arch Cure, Arch Protection, Curse, Greater Heal, Lightning, Mana Drain, and Recall. Fifth Circle includes Blade Spirits, Dispel Field, Incognito, Magic Reflection, Mind Blast, Paralyze, Poison Field, and Summon Creature. Sixth Circle includes Dispel, Energy Bolt, Explosion, Invisibility, Mark, Mass Curse, Paralyze Field, and Reveal. Seventh Circle includes Chain Lightning, Energy Field, Flamestrike, Gate Travel, Mana Vampire, Mass Dispel, Meteor Swarm, and Polymorph. Eighth Circle includes Earthquake, Energy Vortex, Resurrection, Summon Air Elemental, Summon Daemon, Summon Earth Elemental, Summon Fire Elemental, and Summon Water Elemental. Higher circles require more skill and mana.

title: Mage Reagents and Requirements
summary: Reagent types, spell associations, mana costs, skill requirements
content: Mages understand all reagent types and their spell associations: Black Pearl, Blood Moss, Garlic, Ginseng, Mandrake Root, Nightshade, Spider's Silk, and Sulfurous Ash. Each spell requires specific reagent combinations. Mana costs vary by spell circle - First Circle spells cost less mana, Eighth Circle spells cost the most. Skill requirements range from 0-100 Magery skill, with higher circles requiring higher skill. Casting time and difficulty increase with spell circle. Lower circle spells are easier to cast and have lower failure rates. Higher circle spells are more powerful but require more skill, mana, and have higher failure rates. Mages know which reagents are needed for each spell and where to obtain them.

title: Magical Theory and Mechanics
summary: Spell progression, mana regeneration, spell failure, resistance
content: Mages understand magical theory: Spell circles represent progression in magical power and complexity. Mana regeneration occurs naturally over time, with faster regeneration for experienced mages. Spell failure and fizzle mechanics occur when skill is insufficient or when interrupted. Magical resistance types include Physical, Fire, Cold, Poison, and Energy resistance. Spell damage calculations depend on Magery skill, target resistance, and spell circle. Higher skill reduces failure rate and increases damage. Mana efficiency improves with skill - experienced mages use less mana for the same spells. Spell interruption can cause failure. Mages understand the fundamental principles of magic and how to manipulate arcane energies effectively.

title: Advanced Magical Disciplines
summary: Necromancy, Chivalry, Bushido, Ninjitsu, Spellweaving, Mysticism
content: Mages understand advanced magical disciplines: Necromancy involves dark magic, wraith form, and lich form transformations. Chivalry provides Paladin spells and abilities focused on justice and virtue. Bushido offers Samurai magical abilities emphasizing honor and discipline. Ninjitsu provides Ninja magical techniques for stealth and precision. Spellweaving is Elven magic that combines nature and arcane forces. Mysticism offers Mystic spells that blend different magical traditions. Each discipline has unique spells, requirements, and focuses. Some disciplines require specific skills or alignments. Mages know the differences between these magical paths and can advise on which might suit different practitioners.
